explain.rule: Advanced passive forms include: (1) Perfect passives: has/have been + pp (present perfect passive), had been + pp (past perfect passive), will have been + pp (future perfect passive). (2) Continuous passives: is/was being + pp. (3) Modal passives: modal + be + pp (must be done, should have been done). (4) Reporting passives: it is said that / he is believed to / she is thought to be. These are extremely common in formal, academic, and news English.
explain.why_matters: Advanced passives are essential for formal writing, news reporting, and academic English. They let you discuss actions and events without specifying who does them, which is often required in professional contexts.
explain.hebrew_note: Hebrew passive binyanim (נפעל, פועל, הופעל) are common but less systematically used for all these tenses and modals. In English, the passive can apply to virtually any tense and combines with modals in complex ways that require explicit practice.
explain.tip: Key structures to master: was being done (in progress and passive, past), should have been done (should + perfect passive), is said to be / it is believed that (reporting passives common in news). Test: can you say who the agent is? If not, passive is natural here.

good_examples.sentence: The road was being repaired when we drove past.
good_examples.audio_text: The road was being repaired when we drove past.
good_examples.note: Past continuous passive — in progress in the past

good_examples.sentence: The report has been reviewed by the committee.
good_examples.audio_text: The report has been reviewed by the committee.

good_examples.sentence: The building had been abandoned for years before it was renovated.
good_examples.audio_text: The building had been abandoned for years before it was renovated.
good_examples.note: Past perfect passive — before another past event

good_examples.sentence: This should have been done yesterday.
good_examples.audio_text: This should have been done yesterday.
good_examples.note: Modal perfect passive — obligation that wasn't met

good_examples.sentence: It is said that she earns over a million dollars a year.
good_examples.audio_text: It is said that she earns over a million dollars a year.

good_examples.sentence: He is believed to be hiding somewhere in the city.
good_examples.audio_text: He is believed to be hiding somewhere in the city.

good_examples.sentence: The results are expected to be announced tomorrow.
good_examples.audio_text: The results are expected to be announced tomorrow.

good_examples.sentence: The new law is being debated in parliament this week.
good_examples.audio_text: The new law is being debated in parliament this week.

good_examples.sentence: The contract must be signed before the end of the month.
good_examples.audio_text: The contract must be signed before the end of the month.

good_examples.sentence: She was thought to have left the country months ago.
good_examples.audio_text: She was thought to have left the country months ago.

bad_examples.wrong: The road was being repair when we drove past.
bad_examples.correct: The road was being repaired when we drove past.
bad_examples.reason: Passive needs past participle: was being repaired.

bad_examples.correct: This should have been done yesterday.
bad_examples.reason: Modal perfect passive: should have been + pp.

bad_examples.wrong: It is said that she earn over a million.
bad_examples.correct: It is said that she earns over a million.
bad_examples.reason: Reporting passive: it is said THAT + normal clause (present simple: earns).

bad_examples.wrong: The building had abandoned for years.
bad_examples.correct: The building had been abandoned for years.
bad_examples.reason: Past perfect passive needs 'been': had been abandoned.

bad_examples.wrong: He believed to be hiding in the city.
bad_examples.correct: He is believed to be hiding in the city.
bad_examples.reason: Reporting passive: he IS believed to (not 'he believed to').

exercises.question: The road ___ (repair) when we arrived.
exercises.hint: Past continuous passive: was being + pp

exercises.question: The report ___ (review) by the committee yesterday.
This ___ (should/do) weeks ago.
exercises.hint: Modal perfect passive: should + have been + pp

exercises.question: It ___ (say) that he is the wealthiest person in the country.
exercises.hint: Reporting passive: it is said that...

exercises.question: The building ___ (abandon) for decades before they renovated it.
exercises.hint: Past perfect passive: had been + pp

exercises.question: The new policy ___ (debate) in parliament all week.
exercises.hint: Present continuous passive: is being + pp

exercises.question: She ___ (believe/leave) the country.
All applications ___ (must/submit) by Friday.
The results ___ (expect/announce) tomorrow morning.
exercises.hint: Passive + infinitive: are expected to be + pp

exercises.question: The package ___ (deliver) by the time you get home.
exercises.hint: Future perfect passive: will have been + pp

exercises.question: The meeting ___ while we were trying to call.
distractors: was held / has been held / had been held

exercises.question: This mistake ___ have been avoided with better planning.
It ___ that the company will announce new jobs.
The stadium ___ for the past two years.
distractors: is under construction / was under construction / had been constructed

exercises.question: She ___ to be the best candidate for the position.
Find and fix: 'The road was being repair.'
exercises.hint: Past continuous passive needs past participle

exercises.question: Find and fix: 'This should been done yesterday.'
exercises.answer: This should have been done yesterday.

exercises.question: Find and fix: 'He believed to be hiding in the city.'
exercises.answer: He is believed to be hiding in the city.
exercises.explanation: is believed — the verb 'be' is needed.

exercises.question: Find and fix: 'The building had abandoned for years.'
exercises.answer: The building had been abandoned for years.

exercises.question: Put the words in the correct order:
exercises.answer: The bridge was being repaired when we arrived.

exercises.question: Put the words in the correct order:
exercises.answer: This should have been done by Friday.

exercises.question: Put the words in the correct order:
exercises.answer: It is said that she is the wealthiest person in the country.
exercises.hint: Reporting passive: it is said that
exercises.explanation: it is said that + normal clause.

speak.sentence: The building has been converted into luxury apartments.
The report should have been submitted three days ago.
chunks: The report should have been submitted

speak.sentence: It is believed that the suspect is still in the country.
chunks: It is believed that the suspect

speak.sentence: The new motorway is expected to be completed by the end of the year.
chunks: The new motorway is expected to be completed
by the end of the year.

speak.sentence: The road was being repaired so we had to take a different route.
chunks: so we had to take a different route.

speak.sentence: She is thought to have been working on this project for over five years.
chunks: She is thought to have been working on this project
speak.phonetic_hints: 'Thought' = THAWT. Complex passive — take it slowly.

speak.sentence: All decisions must be approved by the board before they can be implemented.
chunks: All decisions must be approved by the board

speak.sentence: The contract had been signed before the dispute arose.
